text:
BOTTOM LINE — Risk-on rotation, growth to cyclicals. XLK and SOXX finally bled as capital rotated into XLV, XLF, and cyclicals while small caps beat large by 1.13pp, pointing to factor rebalancing rather than macro stress. DIA’s 1.66% jump alongside Fear & Greed at 64.3 and VIX slipping to 16.1 underscored buyers pressing exposure but demanding QQQ and IWM downside protection via elevated put-call ratios.

INDICES — VIX 16.1, below its 20d MA and down on the day. DIA +1.66%, IWM +1.51%, SPY +0.38%, QQQ -0.48%, SOXX -2.10%. STYLE line: SMALL > LARGE by 1.13pp. Cross-asset standout: USO -2.92% as oil slid despite risk-on equities.

VOLUME — SPY volume was roughly in-line via the 0.99x index average read, while QQQ volume was implied average with no specific vol_ratio flag. DIA 1.3x and SOXX 1.3x showed attention on Dow cyclicals and semis, while XLF 1.7x and XLV 1.4x flagged heavy sector rotation into financials and healthcare as XLY 0.6x and XLE 0.6x were ignored.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLV Health Care +3.07%, XLF Financials +2.59%, XLRE Real Estate +2.05%, XLI Industrials +1.21%, XLC Comms +0.92%, XLU Utilities +0.53%, XLY Discretionary +0.45%, XLE Energy +0.07%, XLB Materials -0.02%, XLP Staples -0.15%, XLK Technology -1.56%. Top industry ETFs: MSOS +7.59% and ARKG +6.93%; bottom: FNGS -2.42% and USO -2.92%. Money in: healthcare and financials with XLV +3.07% and XLF +2.59%; money out: megacap growth proxies with XLK -1.56% and FNGS -2.42%, i.e., tech and AI leaders funded defensives and cyclicals. Rotation-state label: broadening participation.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR_vol 0.84 with ATM_IV 8.7% and positive skew shows mild protection demand but no panic as VIX trades below its 20d MA. QQQ PCR_vol 1.33 and IWM PCR_vol 1.37 with positive skew confirm concentrated demand for downside in growth and small caps, consistent with profit-taking in SOXX -2.10% and QQQ -0.48%.

CROSS-ASSET — Oil -2.92% via USO weighed on XLE +0.07%, creating a mild equity-energy divergence as broader cyclicals rallied. Precious metals firmed with GLD +0.83% and SLV +1.16% even as VIX fell 3.61% via VIXY, suggesting some investors quietly added hedges outside index vol.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed at 64.3 (Greed), biggest movers: MSOS +7.59% (Cannabis), ARKG +6.93% (Genomics), DRAM -5.75% (Semis). Vol spikes: AVGO 3.5x with -12.59% is clear distribution post-earnings, while XHE 3.1x with +4.19% and ARKG 2.6x with +6.93% look like aggressive accumulation in healthcare and high-beta biotech. Watch MU - via DRAM -5.75% for follow-through in AI memory, XHE +4.19% for confirmation of healthcare leadership, and UNG +3.50% for continuation of the 20d +13.48% natural gas trend.
